serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of hot Americano, typically 240 grams (approximately 8 fluid ounces).

nutrient_source.title: Coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data for Hot Americano is adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry 'Coffee, brewed, prepared with tap water' (FDC ID 171765). An Americano is espresso diluted with hot water, producing a beverage nutritionally equivalent to brewed black coffee. Values are reported per 100g of beverage as consumed.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of coffee beverages — black coffee and espresso-based drinks
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index for Hot Americano is set to 1 (effectively zero) per the Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates. Black coffee is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database with a GI of effectively zero; gi_category is 'low' by convention. Glycemic load per 240g serving is 0.0 due to zero carbohydrate content.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit caffeine intake as it can cause transient blood pressure elevation; consumption of no more than 1–2 cups per day is generally advised pending individual tolerance assessment.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero sugar and negligible glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: Hot Americano contains no added sugar and has a negligible glycemic index, making it generally suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sweeteners or milk.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Caffeine effects on heart rate and blood pressure
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Moderate black coffee consumption may be associated with neutral to modest cardiovascular benefit in healthy adults, but individuals with arrhythmia or established cardiovascular disease should limit intake and seek medical guidance.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Hot Americano is a popular espresso-based coffee beverage prepared by diluting one or two shots of espresso with hot water, resulting in a drink with a similar volume and strength to drip-brewed coffee but with a distinctly richer, more complex flavour profile. Originating in Italy and widely consumed across Singapore and globally, it is served black without milk or sugar. Nutritionally, a hot Americano is an extremely low-calorie beverage, providing approximately 1 kcal per 100g, with negligible protein (0.12g), minimal fat (0.02g), and zero carbohydrates or sugar. It contains no dietary fibre and only trace sodium (2mg per 100g). Its primary bioactive component is caffeine, which provides stimulant effects. Due to its zero sugar content and negligible glycemic index, it is generally well-tolerated by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Caffeine intake warrants monitoring for those with hypertension or cardiovascular conditions. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free.

confidence_reason: Hot Americano is a well-defined and widely documented beverage. Nutrient data for brewed black coffee is available from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 171765), which is an approved primary source. GI data for black coffee is consistent across approved sources and aligns with the zero-carbohydrate nature of the beverage.
notes: Carbohydrate_g, sugar_g, and dietary_fibre_g are naturally zero as black coffee contains no glycemic carbohydrates by nature. Cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 171765. Energy_kcal is reported as 1 kcal per 100g per USDA source (rounded from approximately 1 kcal). Monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this entry. Polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this entry. Trans_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this entry. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in USDA FoodData Central for this entry. Glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule.
